skill.name: azure-reliability
skill.description: Assess Azure Functions and Azure App Service reliability. Checks zone redundancy, storage redundancy, health probes, and multi-region failover, then guides user-confirmed CLI or IaC remediation.
skill.summary: Assess and improve Azure PaaS reliability for Functions and App Service.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 509 potential issues, but review shows most are Markdown examples for Azure CLI, Bicep, Terraform, and documentation links. No prompt injection, malicious exfiltration, or hidden execution logic was found. The skill remains medium risk because it can guide user-confirmed Azure deployments, Terraform applies, storage migrations, and connection string handling.

medium_findings.title: User-Confirmed Cloud Resource Mutation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run azd, Azure deployment, Terraform, and Azure CLI commands after user confirmation. This is legitimate DevOps behavior, but it can change live Azure resources, deploy IaC, and start long-running storage migrations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution behavior is explicit and repeated in the workflow, and it targets live cloud deployment tools. The same sections require user confirmation, so this is operational risk rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Storage Migration Workflow
medium_findings.description: The storage migration guidance includes stopping Function Apps, copying storage data, retrieving a storage connection string, and updating AzureWebJobsStorage. These actions are expected for migration, but mishandling can expose credentials or disrupt production workloads.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly documents connection string retrieval and app setting updates during migration. The risk is contextual and mitigated by warnings and explicit migration steps, so confidence is high but not critical.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm hits occur in infrastructure examples and parameter descriptions, not in executable malware or credential handling logic. No evidence found of weak crypto being used to protect secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed locations are Markdown or IaC guidance, and the surrounding text describes Azure reliability settings. I did not find code that hashes secrets or implements cryptography.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal And Dynamic Function Hits Are Documentation Syntax
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts map to relative Markdown links, and the dynamic function constructor alert maps to a C# Azure Functions attribute example. These are not runtime path traversal or JavaScript dynamic code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines are documentation link syntax and a C# attribute snippet. There is no user-controlled file access or JavaScript Function constructor usage.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Expected Azure And Health Check Endpoints
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings point to Microsoft Learn, Azure Management API, Azure Front Door health checks, or placeholder app URLs. They are not covert exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are standard Azure service endpoints or documentation links used in reliability checks. No evidence found of sending secrets or telemetry to an unrelated domain.

dangerous_patterns.title: Deployment Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can run azd, Terraform, Azure deployment, and az commands as part of remediation. Commands are user-confirmed but can modify infrastructure.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit and intentional for this DevOps skill. It should be disclosed because the blast radius depends on the user subscription and permissions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Connection String Handling In Migration Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents retrieving and applying storage connection strings during Function App storage migration. This should be handled carefully to avoid exposing secrets in logs or chat.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command and app setting names are visible in the migration workflow. The guidance is legitimate, but secret display risk depends on how the assistant presents command output.

content.value_statement: Azure PaaS apps can hide single points of failure across compute, storage, probes, and failover. This skill checks Azure Functions and App Service reliability and guides confirmed fixes through CLI or IaC.
actual_capabilities: Discovers Azure Functions and App Service resources in a subscription, resource group, or app scope.
Checks compute zone redundancy, storage replication, health probes, and global failover readiness.
Produces a feature-focused reliability checklist without numeric scores or grades.
Guides Azure CLI remediation for supported quick wins after explicit user confirmation.
Patches Bicep or Terraform examples for reliability settings and deploy order.
Generates active-passive multi-region guidance with Azure Front Door when requested.
limitations: Covers Azure Functions and Azure App Service only in this version.
Requires Azure authentication and appropriate Reader or Contributor permissions.
Some storage migrations can take hours or days and may require manual planning.
Does not replace full architecture review, load testing, or disaster recovery exercises.

use_cases.title: Pre-production reliability review
use_cases.description: Check a resource group before launch and identify missing zone redundancy, storage redundancy, probes, or failover.

use_cases.title: Operational remediation planning
use_cases.description: Turn reliability gaps into a staged fix plan with cost, downtime, and deployment warnings.

use_cases.title: IaC reliability hardening
use_cases.description: Patch Bicep or Terraform so reliability settings persist across future deployments.

prompt_templates.title: Assess a resource group
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess the reliability posture of my Azure resource group. Focus on Azure Functions and App Service resources.

prompt_templates.title: Find zone redundancy gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether my App Service plans and Function Apps are zone redundant. Show only the resources that need attention.

prompt_templates.title: Patch infrastructure as code
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Bicep or Terraform files and propose reliability patches for zone redundancy, storage redundancy, and health probes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan multi-region failover
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an active-passive multi-region failover plan for my Azure Functions or App Service app using Azure Front Door.

output_examples.input: Assess reliability for one production resource group.
output: A feature-focused checklist showing compute zone redundancy, storage redundancy, health probes, and multi-region failover.
Each row lists affected Azure resources and a clear status such as ON, PARTIAL, or OFF.
The response ends with one confirmation question before any remediation starts.

output_examples.input: Patch my IaC for reliability improvements.
output: A staged remediation plan separates quick wins from storage migration work.
The plan explains expected cost, downtime, restart, and deployment impact.
The assistant asks for confirmation before applying or deploying changes.
A secondary region plan with standby compute, storage, health probes, and Azure Front Door routing.
The plan highlights prerequisites and asks for region and deployment confirmation.

best_practices: Run the assessment before and after each infrastructure change.
Use IaC patching for persistent reliability settings when IaC exists.
Confirm cost, downtime, and migration impact before applying live changes.
anti_patterns: Do not run storage migrations without explicit user approval and a rollback plan.
Do not treat GRS storage as zone redundant for a zone failure scenario.
Do not start multi-region deployment before core single-region reliability gaps are handled.

faq.question: Which Azure services does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Azure Functions and Azure App Service. Other compute services are outside the current deep-dive scope.

faq.question: Can it make changes to live Azure resources?
faq.answer: Yes, it can guide or run Azure CLI and deployment commands after explicit user confirmation.

faq.question: Does it support Bicep and Terraform?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for Bicep and Terraform reliability patches and deployment sequencing.

faq.question: Will it automatically deploy multi-region failover?
faq.answer: No. Multi-region work requires an explicit user request and confirmation before files or resources are changed.

faq.question: Why does storage redundancy matter?
faq.answer: Zone-redundant compute can still fail if its required storage account is not zone redundant.

faq.question: Is this a replacement for disaster recovery testing?
faq.answer: No. It helps identify and remediate configuration gaps, but teams still need failover tests and operational runbooks.
